summary: Full test of new features: Clients CRUD, Client Switcher, PDF/Excel Exports, GST Reports (GSTR-1/GSTR-3B), and RBAC. 20/21 backend tests passed initially; 1 bug found and fixed (GSTR-1 PDF em-dash encoding error). All 21 backend tests now pass. All frontend UI tests pass.

minor.issue: Em-dash character '—' in FinPDF title caused FPDFUnicodeEncodingException with Helvetica font. Fixed by replacing all em-dashes with hyphens in export_utils.py

issues: Period input field shows browser default date picker (-------) instead of a styled component - minor cosmetic issue

critical_code_review_comments: export_utils.py uses Helvetica font which doesn't support Unicode characters like em-dash. Consider using a Unicode font or sanitizing all text in PDF generation
CORS is configured with allow_origins=['*'] and allow_credentials=False - should use explicit origins for production security
GST disclaimer note still says 'flat 18%' even though per-transaction rates are now supported - should update disclaimer text
seed_data_creation: Created 2 test clients via backend tests and 1 via UI (TEST_New Client Ltd, TEST_Verify Client, UI Test Client) - TEST_ clients may still exist in DB
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new features tested: Client CRUD (admin-only), client switcher in sidebar, PDF/Excel exports for Trial Balance/P&L/Balance Sheet/GSTR-1, GST Reports (GSTR-1 with per-tx rates, GSTR-3B with table 3.1 + table 4), RBAC (accountant lacks Clients nav, no client switcher; but has upload/classify access). One bug fixed: em-dash in PDF titles. Viewer role not fully tested (no seed viewer user). Test client IDs from previous run may still exist.
rca_of_issue: GSTR-1 PDF export failed with FPDFUnicodeEncodingException because export_utils.py used em-dash '—' in PDF title strings ('GSTR-1 — Outward Supplies') and fallback values ('—'). The fpdf2 library with Helvetica font only supports Latin-1 charset. Fix: replaced all em-dashes with ASCII hyphens in export_utils.py lines 150, 184, 216, 393, 433.
